data.title: CaliberCos Launches Note Conversion; $3.45M of Notes Exchanged for Common and Preferred Shares
data.description:
CaliberCos launched a note conversion program allowing holders of certain unsecured promissory notes to convert debt into equity. As of April 9, 2026, holders converted $3.45 million of notes, receiving 1,707,900 Class A shares for $1.92 million of notes and 1,529 shares of Series AAA Convertible Preferred Stock for $1.53 million of notes. The subscription agreements include registration rights for the common shares and shares issuable upon conversion of the preferred stock. The program reduces indebtedness and supports balance sheet flexibility.

Agreement details:

  • Agreement type: Note conversion program and equity subscription agreements
  • Counterparty: Note Holders
  • Signed / Effective: Apr 09 2026 / same
  • Duration / Termination: At will
  • Reason: Reduce debt and strengthen balance sheet

data.title: Global Fund expands commitment to improve access to HIV drug
data.description:
By Christy Santhosh

The U.S. and the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria are stepping up efforts to broaden access to Gilead's HIV prevention drug, with a goal of reaching 3 million people by 2028.

Initial deliveries of the drug, lenacapavir, have reached nine African countries, Global Fund said on Tuesday, adding that it would extend the provision of the treatment to 12 additional countries, including Dominican Republic, Fiji, Indonesia, Morocco, Rwanda and Thailand.

In July last year, Global Fund, which is an international partnership, and Gilead finalized their plans to supply lenacapavir to low-income countries, agreeing to provide enough doses to reach up to 2 million people over three years.

Doctors Without Borders (MSF), an independent international medical organization, said it is concerned that this does not address the fundamental barriers keeping this "game-changing" medicine out of reach for many of those who need it most.

"Any expanded access to lenacapavir is a good thing, but reaching only one million more people in three years is a tiny fraction of what's needed to make a real dent in the HIV epidemic." said Tom Ellman, director of MSF’s Southern Africa Medical Unit.

Lenacapavir is a subcutaneous injection given twice a year. It helps to overcome problems associated with daily oral pre-exposure prophylaxis pills (PrEP) such as forgotten doses and difficulties in maintaining a steady supply.

The Global Fund said early program data indicates that uptake is particularly strong among priority populations, including pregnant and breastfeeding women, adolescent girls and young women, and people accessing such drugs for the first time.

Gilead has granted voluntary licenses to several manufacturers to produce generic versions of lenacapavir, helping to pave the way for expanded and more affordable supply in the future, Global Fund said.

data.title: Mergers and acquisitions
data.description:
The following bids, mergers, acquisitions and disposals were reported by 2100 GMT on Tuesday:

** Brazilian steelmaker CSN is pushing ahead with the sale of its cement unit, which could fetch over 10 billion reais ($2 billion), according to two people familiar with the matter.

** Germany's Rheinmetall and Destinus, a Netherlands-based European defence technology company, have agreed to establish a joint venture to supply advanced missile systems, the companies said.

** Shell <SHEL.L> is in advanced talks with Abu Dhabi's state oil company ADNOC to sell its retail fuel stations in South Africa Gas in a deal likely to be valued at about $1 billion, Bloomberg News reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

** BAWAG Group <BAWG.VI> will buy Irish retail lender Permanent TSB <PTSB.I> for 1.62 billion euros ($1.92 billion) in a deal that will widen the Austrian bank's European footprint and allow the Irish government to exit its last bank holding from the financial-crisis-era.

** Yancoal Australia <YAL.AX>, <3668.HK> said it has agreed to acquire an 80% stake in the Kestrel coking coal mine in Queensland for up to $2.4 billion, a move that strengthens its position as one of the country's largest coal miners.

** Amazon.com <AMZN.O> said it would acquire Globalstar <GSAT.O> in an $11.57 billion deal, bolstering its fledgling satellite business as it looks to take on Elon Musk-led bigger rival Starlink.

** German exchange operator Deutsche Boerse <DB1Gn.DE> said it had acquired a 200 million dollar stake in US-based cryptocurrency exchange Kraken.

** China Evergrande Group's liquidators have entered an exclusivity agreement with a selected bidder to sell the developer's majority stake in its property management unit <6666.HK>, an exchange filing showed.

** Avanos Medical <AVNS.N> said it has agreed to be bought by affiliates of American Industrial Partners (AIP) in an all-cash deal worth about $1.27 billion.

** France's Eurofins <EUFI.PA> said it has agreed to sell its electrical and electronic testing unit to UL Solutions for an enterprise value of 575 million euros ($677 million).

** German software and AI provider Nemetschek <NEKG.DE> will acquire construction-management software provider Heavy Construction Systems Specialists (HCSS) from Thoma Bravo, the companies said on Monday.

data.title: SunPower 2025 10-K: Revenue $300.0M, EPS (Basic) $(0.52)
data.description:
SunPower reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $300.0 million and a net loss per share (basic) of $(0.52), reflecting substantial revenue growth driven by acquisitions while still posting a consolidated net loss of $(45.4) million. Gross profit improved to $129.2 million (43% margin), and loss from continuing operations narrowed to $(26.9) million versus prior-year results. The company attributes top-line expansion to full-year contributions from acquired businesses and operational integration.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $300.0 million, increased 176% year-over-year (from $108.7 million).
  • Gross profit: $129.2 million; gross margin 43% (up from $39.5 million and 36% prior year).
  • Operating expenses: $156.1 million, total operating expenses increased 41% driven by sales/marketing and general & administrative expenses.
  • Loss from continuing operations: $(26.9) million, an improvement from $(68.5) million in the prior year.
  • Net loss: $(45.4) million, improved from $(56.5) million prior year.
  • Net loss per share (basic): $(0.52).

Business Highlights

  • Revenue growth and acquisitions: Revenue rise was driven by full-year contributions from acquired SunPower businesses and the integration of Blue Raven assets.
  • Channel expansion: Added a Dealer channel via the Sunder acquisition and strengthened the New Homes and Residential Installation segments, expanding the national sales partner network.
  • Brand and integration: Rebranded to SunPower in 2025 and integrated acquired businesses to build national scale and sales reach.
  • Operational scaling: Expanded installation capacity using a mix of in-house crews and a national installer network, supported by proprietary software for project delivery to improve execution and margins.
  • Product and strategic initiatives: Pursuing battery storage opportunities through a partnership with Enphase and offering turnkey solutions for national sales partners to enhance product mix and margin potential.

Original SEC Filing: SunPower Inc. [ SPWR ] - 10-K - Apr. 14, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: DarkPulse 2025 10-K: Revenue $0.308M, Net Loss $(2.926)M
data.description:
DarkPulse reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $0.308 million and a net loss of $(2.926) million, an improvement from a $(3.894) million loss in 2024. Revenue growth was driven by services from Optilan India and TerraData while gross margin declined versus the prior year. The company is pursuing strategic partnerships and equity financings to fund scaling and serial production.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0.308 million in 2025, up from $0.127 million in 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $0.210 million (68% gross margin) in 2025 versus $0.125 million (98% margin) in 2024; cost of revenues increased mainly due to Optilan India.
  • Net Loss (Net Income): $(2.926) million in 2025, improved from $(3.894) million in 2024.
  • Operating Income: Not reported as a separate line item in MD&A operating expense increases noted, including SG&A and payroll.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not provided as a distinct metric in Part 2 Item 7/Item 8.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix and drivers: Revenue growth was primarily driven by services performed by Optilan India and consolidated services under TerraData, indicating a shift toward service-oriented sales.
  • Channel & product strategy: The company is shifting emphasis from product inventory toward services; hardware manufacturing for serial production is planned with a Sanmina purchase order contingent on capital funding.
  • Product development: Completed development of the Gen-3 BOTDA dark-pulse system and demonstrated units for customer demonstrations.
  • Operational expansion: Expanded global operations through subsidiaries in the UAE, India, Turkey, and the U.S., and consolidated overlapping services into the TerraData unit.
  • Commercial outlook: Management is targeting strategic partners and pursuing equity financings to support serial production, scale sales, and enable broader market deployments.

Original SEC Filing: DarkPulse, Inc. [ DPLS ] - 10-K - Apr. 14, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Blue Water Acquisition III 10-K: $0 Revenue, Net Income $4.67M (no EPS disclosed)
data.description:
Blue Water Acquisition III reported zero operating revenue for the period and does not disclose earnings per share; its net income was $4.667721 million, driven by $5.796563 million of interest and dividend income offset by operating expenses. The company recorded aggregate operating expenses of $1,129,842, resulting in an operating loss before non-operating income. These results reflect activity typical of a recently completed SPAC prior to any business combination.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $0 — the company has not generated operating revenues during the period; income derived from non-operating interest/dividend income.
  • Gross Profit: $0 — no operations or sales occurred so no gross profit was reported.
  • Operating Income: Operating loss implied from aggregate operating expenses of $(1,129,842); operating income figure not separately reported.
  • Net Income: $4,667,721, primarily attributable to $5,796,563 of interest/dividend income less operating expenses.
  • Net Income Per Share: Not reported in the referenced sections.

Business Highlights

  • IPO and Trust Funding: Completed an initial public offering in June 2025 and placed $253 million in trust to be used for a future business combination.
  • Blank-Check Strategy: Operates as a special purpose acquisition company focused on pursuing an initial business combination, targeting biotechnology, healthcare, and technology sectors.
  • Operational Status: No operating revenues to date; generated non-operating interest/dividend income from trust proceeds. Revenue generation is expected only after consummation of a business combination.
  • Governance and Sponsor Transition: Sponsor change and appointment of new board and officers in November 2025; the new sponsor provided working capital support and strategic control to facilitate deal execution.

Original SEC Filing: Blue Water Acquisition Corp. III [ BLUW ] - 10-K - Apr. 14, 2026

data.title: BioNexus Gene Lab 10-K: Revenue $7.42M, EPS $(1.61)
data.description:
BioNexus Gene Lab reported fiscal-year revenue of $7.42 million and a net loss per share of $(1.607), reflecting a 21.9% decline in revenue driven by lower Chemrex volume and foreign exchange translation and a wider operating loss. The company recorded a net loss attributable to common shareholders of $(2.985) million on operating losses that increased 88% year-over-year. Gross profit was $1.085 million with a modest improvement in gross margin to 14.7%.

Financial Highlights

  • Revenue: $7.42 million, decreased 21.9% year-over-year due to lower Chemrex volume and FX translation.
  • Gross Profit: $1.085 million (gross margin 14.7% in 2025 vs 13.7% in 2024).
  • Loss from Operations: $(2.965) million; operating loss increased 88% year-over-year.
  • Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders: $(2.985) million, versus $(1.598) million in the prior year.
  • Earnings per Share — Basic and Diluted: $(1.607); weighted average shares outstanding 1,856,680.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue Trend: Revenue declined roughly 22% year-over-year, primarily from reduced Chemrex sales volume following management changes.
  • Channel & Mix Shift: Chemrex remained the dominant channel (~99.7% of revenue); an improved product mix contributed to a slight uptick in gross margin.
  • Diagnostics and Strategic Moves: Expanded into diagnostics via a Fidelion license and announced plans to advance VitaGuard™ MRD in Southeast Asia through a contemplated 15% equity stake.
  • Operational Remediation: An audit identified governance and control lapses at Chemrex; remediation actions include updated approval workflows and a claims assessment process.
  • Digital Treasury & Partnerships: Announced an Ethereum-focused treasury strategy and a partnership with ML Tech to integrate digital asset and AI capabilities into operations.

Original SEC Filing: BioNexus Gene Lab Corp [ BGLC ] - 10-K - Apr. 14, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: Grades fall as supply concerns ease on hopes of resumption of US and Iran talks
data.description:
Cash crude grades weakened on Tuesday as supply concerns eased on hopes Iran will resume talks with the U.S. and Israel to end the conflict that has shut the Strait of Hormuz.

Mars, the most prominent U.S. sour benchmark, dropped $6.50 to a premium of $2.50 to West Texas Intermediate crude at Cushing on Tuesday, its lowest in more than a month, while other sour grades also fell.

Talks to end the Iran war could resume in Pakistan over the next two days, U.S. President Donald Trump said on Tuesday, after the collapse of weekend negotiations prompted Washington to impose a blockade on Iranian ports.

World oil supply will shrink this year as the war in the Middle East disrupts exports, while demand will also contract, the International Energy Agency said on Tuesday.

Supply will fall by 1.5 million barrels per day this year, the IEA said in its monthly oil market report, as strikes on Middle East energy assets and Iran's effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz hit production and exports.

In the United States, however, crude oil inventories jumped for the third straight week and gasoline stocks also rose, market sources said, citing American Petroleum Institute figures on Tuesday. Distillate inventories fell, the sources added.

Crude stocks rose by 6.1 million barrels in the week ended April 10, the sources said on condition of anonymity. Gasoline inventories rose by 626,000 barrels, while distillate inventories fell by 3.36 million barrels compared to a week earlier, the sources said.

  • Light Louisiana Sweet for May delivery fell $2.50 to rest at a midpoint of a $2.75 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.50 and $3.00 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures

  • Mars Sour dropped $6.50 to a midpoint of a $2.50 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $2.25 and $2.75 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI Midland fell 40 cents to a midpoint of a $1.70 premium and was seen bid and offered between a $1.50 and $1.90 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • West Texas Sour dropped 45 cents to a midpoint of a $1.80 discount and was seen bid and offered between a $2.00 and $1.60 a barrel discount to U.S. crude futures ​

  • WTI at East Houston, also known as MEH, traded between a $2.55 and $2.95 a barrel premium to U.S. crude futures ​

  • ICE Brent June futures fell $4.57 to settle at $94.79 a barrel on Tuesday​.

  • WTI May crude futures fell $7.80 to settle at $91.28 a barrel.

  • The Brent/WTI spread narrowed 8 cents to last trade at minus $6.33, after hitting a high of minus $6.09 and a low of minus $6.98.

data.title: Spok Announces 10% Workforce Cut, Expanded AI Initiatives
data.description:
By Christopher Kuo

Spok Holdings is laying off about 10% of its workforce and cutting costs as it focuses on artificial intelligence initiatives.

The healthcare communications company on Tuesday said it expects the layoffs and other cost-cutting measures to save more than $6 million annually. The company had 421 full-time employees as of Dec. 31, 2025, according to a recent securities filing.

As part of its business realignment, Spok will incorporate AI into its Care Connect Suite, its healthcare communication platform, and it will implement AI technology in its workflows, the company said.

Spok also said its chief operating officer, Michael Wallace, who has been at the company since 2017, will take on the additional role of chief financial officer.

"While any reduction of our leadership team and employee base is a difficult decision, shifting customer preferences has required us to find new ways of driving productivity and efficiency, maintaining profitability, and streamlining our organizational structure to align with our commitments to our customers and stockholders," said Chief Executive Vincent D. Kelly.

The company expects to incur restructuring charges of about $1.6 to $2 million, primarily in the second and third quarters of 2026, Spok said.

Write to Christopher Kuo at chris.kuo@wsj.com

data.title: Smith Midland 2025 10-K: $93.4M Revenue, $2.36 EPS
data.description:
Smith Midland reported fiscal 2025 revenue of $93.446 million and net income of $12.506 million, or $2.36 per share, reflecting stronger margins and lower operating expenses versus 2024. Revenue increased 19% year-over-year, driven by expanded barrier rentals, shipping & installation services and product sales, while EPS rose from $1.45 to $2.36. Management cites improved operational mix and continued demand for infrastructure-related products as drivers of the results.

Financial Highlights

  • Total Revenue: $93.446 million, up $14.938 million (19%) versus 2024.
  • Gross Profit: $26.038 million (as reported).
  • Operating Income: $16.994 million, up $7.095 million (72%).
  • Net Income: $12.506 million, versus $7.675 million in 2024.
  • Net Income Per Share: $2.36 basic and diluted, versus $1.45 in 2024.

Business Highlights

  • Revenue mix shift: The company is moving from barrier sales toward higher-margin barrier rentals, expanding its rental fleet and executing special projects that boosted 2025 results.
  • Product momentum: SlenderWall® production was renewed and Soundwall volumes increased, contributing to product sales growth.
  • Operational investment: Capital spending ramped up for barrier production, attenuators and plant expansions; management plans capital expenditures in excess of $12 million for 2026 to increase capacity.
  • Backlog & outlook: Backlog was approximately $53.1 million as of March 3, 2026; management expects similar product volumes in 2026 with continued infrastructure-driven demand.

Original SEC Filing: SMITH MIDLAND CORP [ SMID ] - 10-K - Apr. 14, 2026

Disclaimer

data.title: FinWise Bancorp to Offer Credit Card With Vera
data.description:
By Kelly Cloonan

FinWise Bancorp is teaming up with financial technology startup Vera to offer an unsecured consumer credit-card program.

The companies said Tuesday the credit card will target prime U.S. consumers as well as near-prime customers, or borrowers who don't quite make the prime category.

Vera will manage the program and purchase receivables from FinWise Bank, which will license its license and regulatory permissions to support the program as its BIN sponsor.

The program will offer unsecured consumer credit cards as both physical and digital cards. Vera's program offers three rewards options that aim to align with customers' individual spending patterns, with no annual fee, the companies said.

Write to Kelly Cloonan at kelly.cloonan@wsj.com

data.title: Robinhood and Strategy Stocks Jump. Why Bitcoin May Finally Be Back. — Barrons.com
data.description:
By George Glover

Crypto-related stocks looked set to be some of the market's best performers on Tuesday, as investors' hopes of a peace deal between the U.S. and Iran gave Bitcoin and other digital assets a much-needed boost.

Shares in online trading platform Robinhood Markets rose 7.9%. Crypto exchange Coinbase Global and major Bitcoin investor Strategy both gained 5.4%.

Crypto transactions account for a large chunk of the three companies' revenue, so their shares tend to move in tandem with Bitcoin. The large-cap digital asset was up 4.6% to $75,004 over the past 24 hours.

The rebound came as investors bet that the conflict in the Middle East could soon end. President Donald Trump said on Monday that the "right people" in Iran still wanted to make a deal, even though talks over the weekend failed.

Bitcoin investors will be hoping that a peace deal can end a brutal crypto winter.

Digital assets have struggled for direction since the first U.S. strikes on Iran. There's no direct link between cryptos and the war, but the geopolitical uncertainty has sapped demand for all sorts of risk assets.

Write to George Glover at george.glover@dowjones.com

This content was created by Barron's, which is operated by Dow Jones & Co. Barron's is published independently from Dow Jones Newswires and The Wall Street Journal.

data.title: Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. Schedules Earnings Release and Conference Call to Announce First Quarter 2026 Results
data.description:
NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--April 14, 2026--

Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. ("GS BDC") announced today that it will report its first quarter ended March 31, 2026 financial results after the market closes on Thursday, May 7, 2026. GS BDC will also host an earnings conference call on Friday, May 8, 2026 at 9:00 am Eastern Time to discuss its financial results.

All interested parties are invited to participate via telephone or the audio webcast, which will be hosted on the Investor Resources section of GS BDC's website at www.goldmansachsbdc.com.

Conference Call Information:

Listen Only Callers:

Domestic: 800-330-6730

International: 646-769-9500

Conference ID: 427709

Q&A Participants:

Domestic: 800-330-6710

International: 646-769-9200

Conference ID: 8110819

All participants are asked to dial in approximately 10-15 minutes prior to the call, and reference "Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc." when prompted.

Replay Information:

An archived replay of the call will be available on our webcast link located on the Investor Resources section of our website at www.goldmansachsbdc.com.

Please direct any questions regarding obtaining access to the conference call to Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. Investor Relations, via e-mail, at acf-gscr@gs.com.

ABOUT GOLDMAN SACHS BDC, INC.

Goldman Sachs BDC, Inc. is a specialty finance company that has elected to be regulated as a business development company under the Investment Company Act of 1940. GS BDC was formed by The Goldman Sachs Group, Inc. ("Goldman Sachs") to invest primarily in middle-market companies in the United States, and is externally managed by Goldman Sachs Asset Management, L.P., an SEC-registered investment adviser and a wholly-owned subsidiary of Goldman Sachs. GS BDC seeks to generate current income and, to a lesser extent, capital appreciation primarily through direct originations of secured debt, including first lien, first lien/last-out unitranche and second lien debt, and unsecured debt, including mezzanine debt, as well as through select equity investments. For more information, visit www.goldmansachsbdc.com. Information on the website is not incorporated by reference into this press release and identification of the website is provided merely for convenience.

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

This press release may contain forward-looking statements that involve substantial risks and uncertainties. You can identify these statements by the use of forward-looking terminology such as "may," "will," "should, " "expect," "anticipate," "project," "target," "estimate," "intend," "continue," or "believe" or the negatives thereof or other variations thereon or comparable terminology. You should read statements that contain these words carefully because they discuss our plans, strategies, prospects and expectations concerning our business, operating results, financial condition and other similar matters. These statements represent GS BDC's belief regarding future events that, by their nature, are uncertain and outside of GS BDC's control. There are likely to be events in the future, however, that we are not able to predict accurately or control. Any forward-looking statement made by us in this press release speaks only as of the date on which we make it. Factors or events that could cause our actual results to differ, possibly materially from our expectations, include, but are not limited to, the risks, uncertainties and other factors we identify in the sections entitled "Risk Factors" and "Cautionary Statement Regarding Forward-Looking Statements" in filings we make with the Securities and Exchange Commission, and it is not possible for us to predict or identify all of them. We undertake no obligation to update or revise publicly any forward-looking statements, whether as a result of new information, future events or otherwise, except as required by law.
